Jeffrey H. Belote and Oriet Cohen-Supple for cross-complainant and moving party: Cushman & Wakefield of California, Inc.
John W. R. Wiley for defendant and opposing party: Baker Hamilton Properties, LLC

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Cross-complainant's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories set #1 is granted in part. With the exception of interrogatory 12.2, defendant is ordered to serve further responses within 15 days of notice of this order. The Court cannot determine from the responses whether the attorney work product privilege applies to any response to 12.3. Defendant is ordered to further respond to interrogatory 12.3 and indicate whether counsel for defendant interviewed any potential witness or instead received a list of potential witnesses who turned over their independently prepared statements. Statements written or recorded independently by witnesses are not work product and must be identified pursuant to the interrogatory.
The Court finds that defendant made unmeritorious objections and failed to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the matter. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1186.30 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
